steps.title: Identify the appositive phrase
steps.content: The phrase “a combination of fiction and memoir, poetry and ______” is an appositive that explains what the book is. It begins with an em dash immediately after the title.

steps.title: Apply the em dash pairing rule
steps.content:
When an appositive is introduced by an opening em dash, it must be closed with a matching em dash before the main verb.

The correct answer (prose—) provides the closing em dash, creating the standard “noun—appositive—verb” pattern.

The other choices either use different punctuation (semicolon, comma) or none at all, which fails to close the em dash pairing.

D is correct.
